session_hint: the redis cpu spike under load burst
question: What did we conclude was causing the Redis CPU spikes during traffic surges?
turns: Redis CPU is hitting 100% during marketing campaign traffic spikes. All cache keys seem to expire at the same second and the resulting misses hammer the database simultaneously.
We preloaded the cache before the campaign using a single script that set every key with the same fixed TTL. So they all expire at exactly the same wall-clock second.

Root cause is a cache stampede from synchronized expiry. Because every key got the identical TTL in a single preload run, they all expire in the same second and every request concurrently misses and queries the database. Fix: add random jitter to TTLs (e.g. base TTL + rand(0..300) seconds) to spread expirations, and use a per-key mutex so only one request recomputes an expired key while others wait or serve stale.
